title: Pach–Tóth Conjecture
statement_text: For every fixed $k$, the maximum number of edges in an $n$-vertex simple topological graph with no $k$ pairwise disjoint edges is at most $O_k(n)$.
context_text: Pach and Tóth [38] proved that every $n$-vertex simple topological graph with no $k \geq 3$ pairwise disjoint edges has at most $O(n \log^{4k-8} n)$ edges, and conjectured a linear bound in $n$ for each fixed $k$. The present paper's Theorem 1.2 substantially improves the upper bound to $n(\log n)^{O(\log k)}$.
paper_contribution: Theorem 1.2 establishes that every $n$-vertex simple topological graph with no $k$ pairwise disjoint edges has at most $n(\log n)^{O(\log k)}$ edges, improving the prior bound of $O(n \log^{4k-8} n)$.
notes: PDF source — year of [38] not recoverable from text.
paper_title: A structure theorem for pseudo-segments and its applications

title: Fox–Sudakov Polynomial Rödl Conjecture
statement_text: Every hereditary family of graphs that is not the family of all graphs satisfies the polynomial Rödl property.
context_text: Fox and Sudakov [20] conjectured a polynomial quantitative version of Rödl's induced-subgraph homogeneity theorem, which would in particular strengthen the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture. The present paper's Theorem 1.1 (homogeneous regularity for intersection graphs of pseudo-segments) implies the polynomial Rödl property for this family via Lemma 2.13.
paper_contribution: Theorem 1.1 establishes the homogeneous regularity property (equivalently the mighty Erdős–Hajnal property) for the hereditary family of intersection graphs of pseudo-segments; Lemma 2.13 then yields the polynomial Rödl property for this family as a consequence.
notes: PDF source — year of [20] not recoverable from text.
paper_title: A structure theorem for pseudo-segments and its applications

title: Optimal disjoint-edges bound in complete simple topological graphs
statement_text: Is it true that every complete $n$-vertex simple topological graph contains $\Omega(n)$ pairwise disjoint edges?
context_text: Aichholzer et al. [2] showed that every complete $n$-vertex simple topological graph always contains $\Omega(n^{1/2})$ pairwise disjoint edges. The paper notes that improving this lower bound to $\Omega(n)$ is an open problem, and Corollary 1.3 provides a new nearly-polynomial lower bound under a density assumption.
notes: Stated in passing as an open problem without a labelled theorem environment; PDF source.
paper_title: A structure theorem for pseudo-segments and its applications
